services.name: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem (CDSE) asset-level STAC catalogue

meta.description: Global land cover map at 10 m resolution derived from Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 observations, produced within the ESA WorldCover project. It is useful in urban contexts to understand how land is distributed between natural areas, agriculture, and built-up zones, for land-use planning and monitoring of urban expansion into surrounding landscapes.

attribution.text: ESA WorldCover project / Copernicus Sentinel data

meta.description: Pan-European land cover dataset produced by the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service. Each pixel represents the dominant land cover class for 2021. It supports urban analysis by enabling comparison of land structure, urbanisation intensity, and landscape composition across European regions.

High-resolution land use dataset for European Functional Urban Areas, produced by combining very high-resolution satellite imagery with photointerpretation and auxiliary geospatial data. It distinguishes detailed urban fabric, infrastructure, industrial areas, and green spaces. It supports the understanding of internal urban structure (residential, industrial, green areas) and the planning of zoning, redevelopment, and green infrastructure strategies.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – Urban Atlas, European Environment Agency (EEA)

categories.label: Discontinuous very low density urban fabric (S.L. < 10%)
Industrial, commercial, public, military and private units
Fast transit roads and associated land

meta.description: Global settlement footprints derived from satellite-based mapping of built-up areas for 2015 and 2019. Built-up detection is performed using multi-source Earth observation data to identify human settlements. It is used to quantify urban expansion, compare settlement growth over time across regions, and identify where urban growth is occurring.

Historical reconstruction of the first year of urbanisation derived from long-term Landsat archives. Built-up areas are detected through temporal analysis of satellite imagery to identify land conversion to urban use. It supports long-term urban planning by showing how and when cities have expanded, useful for understanding historical growth patterns and guiding future development strategies.

attribution.text: © DLR (German Aerospace Center) – World Settlement Footprint Program

meta.description: Global building height estimate at 90 m resolution derived from TanDEM-X radar interferometry combined with optical Earth observation data. It helps to understand vertical development patterns and supports planning decisions related to density, skyline management, and infrastructure capacity.

Estimated building volume per pixel derived from the WSF3D framework combining radar-derived elevation models and land cover information. It assesses urban compactness, built density, and three-dimensional urban intensity. Useful for infrastructure planning and urban growth management.

Pan-European land cover dataset produced by the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service. Each pixel represents the dominant land cover class for 2021. It supports urban analysis by enabling comparison of land structure, urbanisation intensity, and landscape composition across European regions.

Global settlement footprints derived from satellite-based mapping of built-up areas for 2015 and 2019. Built-up detection is performed using multi-source Earth observation data to identify human settlements. It is used to quantify urban expansion, compare settlement growth over time across regions, and identify where urban growth is occurring.

Historical reconstruction of the first year of urbanisation derived from long-term Landsat archives. Built-up areas are detected through temporal analysis of satellite imagery to identify land conversion to urban use. It supports long-term urban planning by showing how and when cities have expanded, useful for understanding historical growth patterns and guiding future development strategies.

attribution.text: © DLR (German Aerospace Center) – World Settlement Footprint Program

meta.description: Urban Heat Island (UHI) intensity with respect to the local rural mean land surface temperature, for Summers between 2021 and 2025.
Urban Heat Island (UHI) intensity with respect to the local rural mean land surface temperature, averaged over the summer months for each year.
Presence of street trees, showing areas where there is most cooling.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – Urban Atlas 2012

meta.description: High-resolution population distribution from WorldPop modelling, combining census data and satellite-derived settlement information. It supports identification of population concentration and urban pressure zones.
High-resolution land use and land cover map for European functional urban areas, providing detailed classification of urban and peri-urban zones for the 2018 reference year.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – Urban Atlas, European Environment Agency (EEA)

categories.label: Discontinuous very low density urban fabric (S.L. < 10%)
Industrial, commercial, public, military and private units
Fast transit roads and associated land

meta.description: Fine particulate matter concentration derived from Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service reanalysis and atmospheric chemistry models. It supports assessment of urban air pollution exposure and associated health risks.

Surface nitrogen dioxide concentration derived from CAMS atmospheric composition modelling combining satellite observations and chemical transport models. It supports identification of traffic-related pollution hotspots in urban areas.
Surface level No2 for Oslo at the course resolution measured by the S5P/TROPOMI-derived instrument
Data from the previous layer downscaled to 1km resolution using modeling.
Land surface temperatures over Austria in the period June 1st and June 24th for 3 years (2022-24), as measured by the ECOSTRESS instrument mounted on the International Space Station.
Rooftop footprints for major Austrian cities, supporting urban morphology and building-scale spatial analysis.

attribution.text: Brockmann Consult – xcube GeoDB (Austria Rooftops Dataset)

meta.description: Presence of street trees, showing areas where there is most cooling.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – Urban Atlas 2012

meta.description: Percentage of tree canopy cover per pixel derived from Copernicus High Resolution Layers using Sentinel-2 imagery. It is relevant for evaluating urban greening distribution, shading potential, heat mitigation strategies and ecological structure in cities.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – High Resolution Layer Tree Cover Density

meta.description: Classification of tree canopy into broadleaved and coniferous types derived from Copernicus High Resolution Layers using Sentinel-2 spectral information and vegetation modelling. Useful for understanding urban forest composition and differences in vegetation structure and seasonality, supporting biodiversity planning and seasonal maintenance strategies.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – High Resolution Layers

meta.description: Spatial distribution of grass-dominated surfaces derived from Sentinel-2 probability layers within Copernicus High Resolution Layers. It separates herbaceous vegetation from other land cover types.  It supports in identifying permeable green areas that contribute to drainage, recreation spaces, and ecological connectivity.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – High Resolution Layers

meta.description: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index, derived from Sentinel-2 optical reflectance using red and near-infrared bands. It is updated daily to capture vegetation greenness and photosynthetic activity. It supports urban environmental monitoring by detecting vegetation stress, seasonal dynamics, and urban–rural vegetation gradients. Useful for early detection of drought or heat stress impacts on urban vegetation.

Leaf Area Index derived from Sentinel-2 observations using radiative transfer modelling. It estimates vegetation canopy density as leaf area per unit ground surface. It characterizes canopy structure, density, and seasonal variability.

Forest biomass volume per hectare derived from a machine learning model combining Sentinel-1 radar, Sentinel-2 optical data, and auxiliary geospatial datasets. It quantifies surrounding forest resources and carbon storage.

attribution.text: © European Space Agency (ESA), Forest Classification Model (FCM) – Growing Stock Volume 2020

meta.description: Base layer: Open StreetMap

Change layer of surface sealing derived from Copernicus High Resolution Imperviousness products based on Sentinel-2 time series classification. It captures increases, decreases, or stability in impervious surfaces. It is relevant to monitor soil sealing trends, urban densification, and loss of permeable surfaces, which are important for drainage and climate adaptation.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – High Resolution Layer Imperviousness Change 2018–2021

meta.description: Fine particulate matter concentration derived from Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service reanalysis and atmospheric chemistry models. It supports assessment of urban air pollution exposure and associated health risks.

Surface nitrogen dioxide concentration derived from CAMS atmospheric composition modelling combining satellite observations and chemical transport models. It supports identification of traffic-related pollution hotspots in urban areas.
Surface ozone concentration estimated from atmospheric chemistry modelling within the CAMS system. It supports evaluation of photochemical pollution dynamics in urban atmospheres.
Aerosol optical depth at 550 nm derived from CAMS atmospheric aerosol modelling representing dust load in the atmosphere. It supports analysis of episodic dust events affecting urban air quality.
Flood defence capacity expressed as return periods for which protection systems are designed. It supports assessment of urban flood preparedness and infrastructure resilience.
Flood scenarios generated from hydrological and hydraulic models within EFAS and Copernicus EMS. It supports identification of areas exposed to potential inundation under different climate scenarios.
Probabilistic flood occurrence layer derived from hydrological modelling of river discharge and historical flood events. It supports risk-based urban planning and flood exposure assessment.

Near real-time flood extent mapping derived from satellite imagery during flood events using rapid classification methods. It supports operational response and emergency management in urban flood events.

Fire occurrence frequency derived from MODIS burn scar detections (MCD64A1) aggregated over time. It supports risk assessment and identification of areas repeatedly affected by fires, especially near urban–wildland interfaces.

Spatial probability model of landslide occurrence derived from terrain, geology, land cover, and environmental predictors. It supports the identification of unstable slopes to avoid risky development in vulnerable areas.

High-resolution population distribution from WorldPop modelling, combining census data and satellite-derived settlement information. It supports identification of population concentration and urban pressure zones.
Combined index of air pollution and population exposure derived from Copernicus atmospheric models and demographic datasets. It supports assessment of health risk distribution in urban environments.
Population exposure to flood scenarios derived by overlaying hydrological flood models with gridded population data. It supports estimation of human impact under flood scenarios.
Generalised European road network derived from OpenStreetMap data. It supports urban accessibility analysis and connectivity assessment between settlements and regions.

Selected infrastructure features extracted from OpenStreetMap and generalised for regional-scale mapping of key urban structures. It supports identification of functional urban nodes, critical infrastructure distribution, useful for emergency planning and urban management..
City-level heat indicators derived by combining Sentinel-3 land surface temperature and ERA5 reanalysis air temperature. Metrics include anomalies, heatwave duration, and extreme heat events.

attribution.text: Copernicus Climate Data Store / ECMWF ERA5 + Sentinel-3

meta.description: Topsoil organic carbon content (0–20 cm) derived from spatial modelling of soil observations and environmental predictors calibrated with reference datasets. It identifyies areas with healthy or degraded soils, important for land conservation and urban expansion planning.
Drought conditions derived from the European Drought Observatory using hydrological and meteorological indicators. It supports monitoring of water stress affecting urban and agricultural systems.

Deviation of soil moisture from long-term averages derived from hydrological modelling and data assimilation systems. It helps detect abnormal dry or wet conditions affecting agriculture and urban green spaces.
A pan-European spatially consistent and seamless, detailed land cover inventory for the 2021 reference year, for each pixel showing the dominant land cover among the 11 basic land cover classes.
High-resolution land use and land cover map for European functional urban areas, providing detailed classification of urban and peri-urban zones for the 2018 reference year.

attribution.text: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) – Urban Atlas, European Environment Agency (EEA)
